result:
All the intended files changed (plus auto-generated `next-env.d.ts`, `tsconfig.json`, `package-lock.json` from the install/build).

## Summary

The site was a mid-pivot SaaS with a broken migration: content had moved from the old product ("InboxZap", an email-cleanup tool) to the new one ("Lumina", an AI meeting assistant), but the technical SEO surface was never updated to match — actively suppressing organic traffic. Fixes made:

1. **Brand/content mismatch** — `layout.tsx` metadata (title, description, OG/Twitter tags) still said "InboxZap" while the page content was entirely about "Lumina." A mismatched title/snippet vs. landing content tanks CTR and relevance signals. Rewrote metadata to match the actual product, added canonical URLs and `metadataBase`.
2. **Dead nav/CTA links** — `/features` and `/pricing` were linked from nav, the sitemap, and the homepage CTA button but had no pages (404s). Built both out with real, on-topic content.
3. **Broken blog** — the blog index fetched from `/api/posts`, which doesn't exist (no `app/api` dir at all), so it always errored for users and crawlers. The 12 historic post URLs (`/blog/post-1`…`post-12`) that still carry GSC impressions had no pages either. Rebuilt the blog as static-generated pages with real content on the current product's topic (AI meeting notes), and added 301 redirects from the old dead post URLs to `/blog` so that residual search traffic doesn't hit a 404.
4. **Missing OG image** — `og:image` pointed to a nonexistent file; replaced with a generated `opengraph-image.tsx` (Next's built-in convention).
5. **Missing structured data** — added `SoftwareApplication` JSON-LD site-wide and `FAQPage` JSON-LD on the homepage (the `/faq` redirect now actually lands on a real FAQ section, which didn't exist before).
6. **Sitemap** — now includes `/features`, `/pricing`, and all real blog posts instead of a stubbed TODO.

Verified with a full `next build` (all 12 routes render, zero errors) and a local `next start` — every page returns 200, redirects resolve correctly, and the sitemap/OG image/robots.txt all serve as expected. (Aside: `npm install` flagged that Next.js 14.2.5 has a known CVE — unrelated to this task but worth a version bump when convenient.)
